scope.summary: Closes A5-secreview by adding the dashboard-origin threat-model addendum and linking it from the README, threat model, and A5 plan entry.
out_of_scope: No dashboard authentication or operator-route implementation code was changed by this close.
No Beads deferral, promotion, or cross-repo tracker cleanup was performed.
Unrelated shared-checkout dirty files were not used as evidence.

entry_points.name: security review addendum
entry_points.trace: `git show 7103aef84a95fb0c0108b987238ef588e6b97b35:docs/dashboard-origin-threat-model-addendum.md` contains the A5 fetch-first plus strict-Origin argument, the refusal of literal Origin:null, the JSON/CSRF/action-ticket/SameSite cookie layers, the unchanged body-only pairing contract, the secret-free same-origin pairing referrer policy, and the ambient-cookie-vs-bearer distinction from the agent-mail reference.

entry_points.name: threat-model cross-reference
entry_points.trace: `git show 7103aef84a95fb0c0108b987238ef588e6b97b35:docs/threat-model.md` links the addendum from companion documents and T12; T12 names same-origin JSON fetch, route-scoped tickets, and literal Origin:null refusal, with evidence test names for served pairing and malicious dashboard-origin cases.

entry_points.name: A5 and README links
entry_points.trace: `git show 7103aef84a95fb0c0108b987238ef588e6b97b35:README.md` and `git show 7103aef84a95fb0c0108b987238ef588e6b97b35:docs/plan/PLAN_0_9_1_FIELD_HARDENING.md` link docs/dashboard-origin-threat-model-addendum.md from the dashboard pairing contract and the A5 security-review requirement.

entry_points.name: dashboard action origin test
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core malicious_page_cannot_trigger_dashboard_gated_action -- --nocapture` exited 0; the selected unit test passed and proves malicious/cross-origin and literal Origin:null dashboard action POSTs do not reach dispatch while a same-origin JSON POST with CSRF and action ticket succeeds.

entry_points.name: served pairing origin and ticket tests
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core served_dashboard_pairing -- --nocapture` exited 0; 3 selected unit tests passed, covering secret-free served pairing URL/form, query ?ticket refusal without consumption, Origin:null refusal without consumption, one-time replay refusal, and the deliberate same-origin/no-referrer header split.

entry_points.name: authenticated-principal origin test
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-core dashboard_operator_auth_enforces_origin_even_for_authenticated_principals -- --nocapture` exited 0; the selected unit test passed and proves browser Origin/Sec-Fetch checks run even when an authenticated principal is already present.

entry_points.name: doc whitespace check
entry_points.trace: `git diff --check -- README.md docs/threat-model.md docs/dashboard-origin-threat-model-addendum.md docs/plan/PLAN_0_9_1_FIELD_HARDENING.md` exited 0 before the documentation commit.

limitations: The first attempted scoped cargo test was refused by the repo guard because the inherited environment pointed CARGO_TARGET_DIR at /home/durakovic/.cache/cargo-target; all successful cargo evidence was rerun with CARGO_TARGET_DIR unset so it used the checkout target directory.
This is a written security review and cross-reference close; it relies on already-landed A5 implementation tests and does not assert a full workspace test run in the shared checkout.
